channel.title: Relational Computing Terms on emsenn.net
channel.description: Recent content in Relational Computing Terms on emsenn.net

item.title: Operator
item.description: A relational-computing operator is a computing operator specialised to the relational substrate: its source and target are graphs of relations — or regions thereof — rather than stored values. Where a generic computing operator transforms inputs of arbitrary type to outputs of arbitrary type, a relational-computing operator’s inputs and outputs are necessarily relational structures, and its action is correspondingly restricted to the three relational primitives: insertion of a relation, retraction of a relation, and traversal along relations.

item.title: Relational Computer
item.description:
A relational computer is a computer whose primary substrate is the relation. Its data is encoded as associations between entities, and its computation is the addition, removal, and traversal of those associations in a graph. The relational computer takes relationality as its organizing ontology.

¶Substrate and identity

In a relational computer, an entity is constituted by the relations it participates in, not by its location in memory or the value stored at that location. Identity is positional in the graph rather than locational. Two entities are equal when their relational structure is the same; an entity persists when its relations persist; an entity is observed by traversing the edges incident to it.
